LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, NOTICE OF MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT UPON THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT UPON THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS ON SFMTA IS DENIED AS MOOT. WHETHER OR NOT SFMTA IS AN INDEPENDENT PUBLIC ENTITY OR WAS PROPERLY SERVED, THE CITY CONCEDES IT IS "THE PROPER DEFENDANT," AND THE ACTION THEREFORE MAY PROCEED AGAINST IT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's demurrer is overruled in its entirety. If the complaint states a cause of action under any theory, regardless of the title under which the factual basis for relief is stated, that aspect of the complaint is good against a demurrer." (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38.) While the City is correct that it is immune from general claims of negligence, Plaintiff sufficiently states a cause of action for a dangerous condition of public property under Government Code 835, including alleging that the City had actual or constructive notice of the unsafe condition but negligent failed to remedy or warn of it despite having sufficient time to take corrective measures. (Compl. 40, 43, 45, 46.) "The existence of a dangerous condition is ordinarily a question of fact . . . .'" (Cerna v. City of Oakland (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1340, 1347.)

The City's argument that spilled liquid constitutes a "transitory condition" which can never constitute a dangerous condition of public property is unsupported by the cases it cites and lacks merit. A store owner may be liable for injuries to a business invitee from a dangerous condition on its premises if it fails to inspect the premises within a reasonable period of time, which is sufficient to allow an inference that the condition was on the floor long enough to give the owner the opportunity to discover and remedy it. (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1206-1211 [slip and fall by shopper on puddle of milk].) The City offers no reason the same rule should not apply to public entities. (Cf. Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829 ["Because there was no evidence that the District had notice of the allegedly dangerous condition, the superior court correctly ruled that the District could not be liable under section 835, subdivision (b)."] [slip and fall on slice of lunchmeat on floor of school district building].) Further, claims for emotional distress may be asserted in a dangerous condition claim. "Section 835 encompasses that type of injury." (Delta Farms Reclamation Dist. v. Sup. Ct. (1983) 33 Cal.3d 699, 711, internal citations omitted.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Upon The San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint Upon The San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to quash service of the summons on SFMTA is denied as moot. Whether or not SFMTA is an independent public entity or was properly served, the City concedes it is "the proper defendant," and the action therefore may proceed against it.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
